paper_metadata.title: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
paper_metadata.abstract: Text-to-image generation has traditionally focused on finding better modeling assumptions for training on a fixed dataset. These assumptions might involve complex architectures, auxiliary losses, or side information such as object part labels or segmentation masks supplied during training. We describe a simple approach for this task based on a transformer that autoregressively models the text and image tokens as a single stream of data. With sufficient data and scale, our approach is competitive with previous domain-specific models when evaluated in a zero-shot fashion.

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a zero-shot text-to-image generation framework using a sparse transformer decoder with row, column, and convolutional attention masks, trained on a large-scale text-image dataset to generate images from captions without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: 250 million text-image pairs collected from the internet
extraction_data.application: Image generation from text descriptions, creative design, content creation
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Text-to-image generation via latent diffusion modeling
extraction_data.extension: Uses a logit-Laplace distribution for pixel reconstruction, which is bounded in (0,1) and resolves the mismatch between pixel value ranges and unbounded distribution support.

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces a zero-shot text-to-image generation framework using a latent diffusion model with a pretrained text encoder and a diffusion-based image decoder. The model enables high-fidelity image synthesis from natural language descriptions without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.application: Image generation from text prompts, creative design, content creation
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Text-to-image generation via latent diffusion modeling
extraction_data.extension: Uses a latent diffusion model with a pretrained CLIP text encoder for zero-shot text-to-image translation.
